items.title: In most places in the world, power from new renewables is now cheaper than power from new fossil fuels. Why did renewables become so cheap so fast? Th...
items.description_html:
In most places in the world, power from new renewables is now cheaper than power from new fossil fuels.

Why did renewables become so cheap so fast?

The answer: learning curves. For renewables and other technologies that follow learning curves, with each doubling of the cumulative installed capacity their price declines by the same fraction.

The price of electricity from fossil fuels, however, does not follow learning curves.

This means we should expect that the price difference between expensive fossil fuels and cheap renewables will become even larger in the future.

items.title: Over the last two decades, India has made consistent progress in improving access to sanitation, outpacing several countries that were at similar leve...
items.description_html:
Over the last two decades, India has made consistent progress in improving access to sanitation, outpacing several countries that were at similar levels or ahead at the time.

One key aspect of access to sanitation has been the use of toilets, and the decrease in open defecation. The proportion of Indians defecating in the open has dropped from 70% in 1993 to 19% by 2021. However, toilet usage varies significantly across states.

Northern and north-eastern states, record high toilet usage rates of over 90%, along with Kerala achieving 100%. Conversely, Bihar has the highest share of households not using any toilet facility at nearly 40%, followed by Jharkhand and Odisha, where around one in three households lack toilet facilities.

Over half the Indian households that are forced to defecate in the open reside in four states: Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu.

#Toilets #RuralIndia #India #WashData #WASH #DataForIndia

items.title: Measles vaccination has saved an estimated 94 million lives globally since 1974. Of those, 92 million were children. The chart shows the number of liv...
items.description_html:
Measles vaccination has saved an estimated 94 million lives globally since 1974. Of those, 92 million were children.

The chart shows the number of lives saved by different childhood vaccines in the last 50 years. These estimates come from Andrew Shattock and colleagues and are based on global data on diseases, causes of death, vaccination rates, and vaccine efficacy.

Measles vaccines rank the highest in the total number of lives saved.

Measles is especially contagious and deadly. The virus depletes immune cells, making it harder to fight off measles and making other infections much more life-threatening. It can also erase immune memory to infections and vaccines that children have already encountered.

Before vaccines, almost all children caught measles, and it was a common cause of disability and death. With high measles vaccination rates, millions of lives are saved globally each year.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by Saloni Dattani.)

India exported $150 billion worth of software or Information and Communication Technology (ICT) services in 2022-23, making it the biggest category, outdoing even petroleum products. items.title: In the early 2000s, less than 8/10 Indians had access to basic drinking water, lower than the world average and even our neighbours in Sri Lanka. Two ...
items.description_html:
In the early 2000s, less than 8/10 Indians had access to basic drinking water, lower than the world average and even our neighbours in Sri Lanka. Two decades later, nearly 95% of Indian households now have access to basic drinking water, a rate of progress that has surpassed the world's average.

However, when it comes to tap water, rural India still lags behind, with only 39% of households using it as their primary source of drinking water compared to 68% in urban regions. Hand pumps and tube wells remain the primary source of drinking water for nearly half of rural households. This reliance on hand pumps and tube wells in rural regions is especially pronounced in states like Uttar Pradesh (95%), Bihar (85%), and West Bengal (75%). 💧

Administrative data from a government scheme indicates that 60% of rural households have a tap water connection in their homes by the middle of 2023. The NSS survey data finds that only 30% of rural households used piped water in their own homes or yards as their primary source of drinking water as of 2023. 🔗

#Water #SurveyData #WashData #WASH #DataForIndia

items.title: Understanding who uses mobile phones in India and how they use them requires going beyond the data on registered SIM cards. While data on registered m...
items.description_html:
Understanding who uses mobile phones in India and how they use them requires going beyond the data on registered SIM cards.

While data on registered mobile SIM cards may hint at the scale of penetration, it does not capture how many people are using phones and who they are; some people and businesses may have more than one SIM card, while others may not be using phones at all. 📱

To understand how many people actually use phones and who they are, we looked at the latest data (released in Oct, 24) from a nationally representative sample survey of over a million people conducted by the Indian government about, among other things, their mobile phone and internet usage.

In 2023, 85% of adults or about 850 million people had used a mobile phone in the last three months. About 8/10 women and 9/10 men had used mobile phones recently. A majority of Indian phone users, particularly among women, share their phone with a family member, and are not exclusive users of the device they use. 🤝🏽 

Among those who do not use phones, is not a question of a lack of access. Nine in ten people who do not use a phone live in households that own a mobile phone.

#MobilePhone #Internet #Survey #GovernmentData #DataForIndia

items.title: While India boasts very successful elementary education enrolment, the country still faces challenges in enrolment for higher education. As of 2022, o...
items.description_html:
While India boasts very successful elementary education enrolment, the country still faces challenges in enrolment for higher education.

As of 2022, over 40 million young Indians (age 18-23) were enrolled in higher education programmes, but India’s enrolment rates is still below the global average. In the early 1990s, India and China had similar enrolment rates for both primary and higher education. However, in the decades since, China has made significant progress. Over 70% of young Chinese adults are enrolled in higher education, compared to just over 30% in India.

There are also sharp differences between states within India. Richer states have a higher proportion of graduates:
Kerala - Nearly 1 in 4 people hold a degree
Assam - Fewer than 1 in 20

#Education #HigherEducation #DataForIndia

items.title: L&T Acquiring stakes in E2E is not only critical for the company and investors but also for the whole startup ecosystem of India. @tarundua81's vision...
items.description_html:
L&T Acquiring stakes in E2E is not only critical for the company and investors but also for the whole startup ecosystem of India. @tarundua81's vision and execution has surpassed expectations beyond everything. It's a testament that tech-founders driven companies can do wonders when vision meets execution and tailwinds then takes them into the next orbit. I remember how some entitled folks reacted to a small outage that happened and the way Tarun has handled scheme of things is phenomenal. The concall quality, the dept of discussion has only gone up.
Though I was one of the early boarders, the LT aspect takes things into totally another universe now! Wishing all investors and management at @e2enetworks a great success.

items.title: Dal bhaat: Rajma ka madra with local sticky rice From Himachal Pradesh In episode 5 of the Green Kitchen, food historian and critic Professor Pushpesh...
items.description_html:
Dal bhaat: Rajma ka madra with local sticky rice From Himachal Pradesh
In episode 5 of the Green Kitchen, food historian and critic Professor Pushpesh Pant and CSE's Vibha Varshney discuss a rice and legume combination from Himachal Pradesh.
In Himachal, chickpeas, pulses, and rajma are usually cooked in Madra. Most of Himachali cuisine is sattvic and vegetarian, therefore, we're not even going to use onions or garlic today. Usually, Madra is served in a Dham or festive celebration. This is enjoyed with the local Sticky Rice.
Join Professor Pant as he delves into the history of this rice and beans variety along with their connection to the environment. Enjoy Dal bhaat - Rajma ka madra with local sticky rice with us!
The Green Kitchen is a show that celebrates the link between nature, nutrition and livelihood. It showcases foods sourced from local biodiversity
#food #nutrition #health #rice #rajma #himachalpradesh #Himachal #greenkitchen #downtoearth #research #foodshow

items.title: E2E became 25x today from the buying at 169. And, in all ways, it has been more surreal than I could ever dream of. I got in with a large position siz...
items.description_html:
E2E became 25x today from the buying at 169. And, in all ways, it has been more surreal than I could ever dream of. I got in with a large position size to begin with and the rally up in such a short time was sweet until one time but then it reminded me of countless experiences of the past in which I ended up giving humongous gains back just because I didn't sell. Honestly speaking, I had doubts about the company being able to raise funds and I am happy to be proven wrong. The sheer sentiments around compute is beyond euphoria. And just like it happens with everyone - what you sell goes up, what you don't, goes down. I have zero regrets on selling because I wanted to keep the gains and that's the largest ever I have made in my investing career in absolute terms. Cheers to those who joined along and made bumper returns.
More than money, it's the countless messages I receive each day, thanking for this and how many have made life changing returns, makes me happy beyond measures. But I give much credit to luck too. Buying is in our hands, but when and how much it goes up isn't. Cheers to everyone and Wishing all a very happy, healthy and prosperous Diwali 🎇 🪔

items.title: 🚨Early Access To Exclusive Swiggy Report🚨 Swiggy is going for IPO at Rs 95,000 cr valuation - 8.5 times its trailing FY24 revenue. Rival Zomato ...
items.description_html:
🚨Early Access To Exclusive Swiggy Report🚨

Swiggy is going for IPO at Rs 95,000 cr valuation - 8.5 times its trailing FY24 revenue.

Rival Zomato has a market cap of Rs 218,257 cr - 18 times its trailing FY24 revenue.

So, Swiggy is giving a 50% valuation multiple discount.

Why❓

Swiggy won the first leg of the race for food and quick deliveries between 2015 and 2021, but now Zomato holds the top position in both segments.

More importantly, Zomato has also been able to reach profitability and break-even across both these segments.

This raises important questions about Swiggy’s future. If Swiggy is to outpace Zomato in the coming decade, incremental gains in market share or marginal growth will not be enough.

The company will need to deliver new products that significantly expand its total addressable market (TAM).

While a publicly listed Zomato chases aggressive targets, Swiggy continues to move carefully in every area, whether monetisation or cost-cutting.

All of this goes back to the very different DNAs of the two companies, which are driven by their founders.

In this extensively researched report, @thearc_hq provides an exclusive profile of Swiggy’s reclusive founder, Sriharsha Majety, based on over a dozen interviews.

We also explore how Swiggy’s top management team is structured, how its product strategy differs from Zomato’s, and what the future may hold.

Link - https://docsend.com/v/7v8z5/swiggyfilesbythearctm

items.title: People tend to think there are more immigrants in their country than there really are. In the United States, for example, the average person believes ...
items.description_html:
People tend to think there are more immigrants in their country than there really are.

In the United States, for example, the average person believes that 33% of people were not born there. However, official estimates from government agencies show that the figure is just 15% — less than half as many.

In Japan, people guess that 10% of the population are immigrants, but the accurate figure is around 2%. For every 5 immigrants people think exist, there is only one in reality.

As the chart shows, this mismatch is common everywhere. The data comes from the 2023 Ipsos Perils of Perception report.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by Simon van Teutem.)

items.title: India’s food inflation has averaged about 7% over the last two years and more than 6% over the past four years. Vegetable prices have seen double dig...
items.description_html:
India’s food inflation has averaged about 7% over the last two years and more than 6% over the past four years.

Vegetable prices have seen double digit inflation for 22 months in the last four years.

Yet, the cooking cost allowance for the crucial mid day meal scheme has not been raised since Oct 2022, forcing teachers to cut back on meals given to under privileged students. Many young kids depend on this meal for their best shot at nutrition.

Reporting with Reuters’ @DashJatin and @saurabhsherry

Do read: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-high-food-inflation-leaves-less-lunch-boxes-poor-school-children-2024-10-11/

items.title: How far apart are the incomes of the rich and poor in different countries? The chart shows how incomes are spread in several countries in different wo...
items.description_html:
How far apart are the incomes of the rich and poor in different countries?

The chart shows how incomes are spread in several countries in different world regions. The data comes from the excellent Luxembourg Income Study.

Blue points show the monthly after-tax income of someone who falls just inside the richest 10% of their country's population. Red points show the income of someone who falls just inside the poorest 10%.

To allow for comparisons, all incomes are shown in international dollars, which account for differences in cost of living across countries.

The ratio between these two numbers gives us a measure of inequality known as the “P90/P10 ratio”.

In Norway, a country with very low inequality, this ratio is around 3. A person just inside the richest 10% has a monthly income of $5,490 — a little more than 3 times the $1,760 earned by someone just inside the poorest 10%.

In the United States, inequality is much higher, and the ratio is twice as large — around 6. The chart shows that the richest 10% are much richer than those in Norway, with incomes above $7,440. But, the poorest are also poorer, with incomes of less than $1,240.

In many countries, the ratio is between 4 and 6. But in the most unequal countries, it is much higher.

In South Africa, the ratio is 22. Those in the bottom decile are among the poorest people in the world, living on less than $110 per month. In contrast, the richest 10% earn $2,490 or above — higher than the incomes of half the UK population and nearly a third of the US population.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @JoeHasell.)

items.title: Mega Wealth Creator in the Last 4 Years Cheat Code for Multibagger: - Starting Valuations: 10-20 P/E Lower the valuations, higher the upside - Revenue...
items.description_html:
Mega Wealth Creator in the Last 4 Years

Cheat Code for Multibagger:

- Starting Valuations: 10-20 P/E
Lower the valuations, higher the upside

- Revenue Growth: 20-25%
This means 2x revenue in 3-4 years

- Levers for Margin Expansion
Operating leverage must play out
PAT growth > Revenue growth

If REV doubles, PAT must increase by at least 3-5x

Observe the pattern
Do not overpay
If starting valuations are higher, the upside gets capped

The best time to add stocks is during panic situations
Always be prepared with a watchlist of stocks
In a correction of 20-25%, a 25 P/E stock comes down below 20x, that's the opportunity
Always be ready

Have a portfolio of at least 20 stocks across 10 sectors for adequate diversification

Trying to follow the same strategy—easier said than done ;)

Add only those stocks which fit this framework.
Read concalls, investor presentations, management interviews & keep tracking every quarter

One multibagger with the right allocation can change your life
Bookmark it

#investing #stockmarkets

items.title: New petrol and diesel cars are becoming a rarity in Norway. In 2023, 93% of new cars sold in the country were electric. This is shown in the chart, ba...
items.description_html:
New petrol and diesel cars are becoming a rarity in Norway.

In 2023, 93% of new cars sold in the country were electric. This is shown in the chart, based on data from the International Energy Agency.

This is a rapid increase from a decade ago when just 6% of new cars were electric.

Here, “electric cars” include fully battery-electric and plug-in hybrid cars (which have a smaller battery and also have a combustion engine).

But in Norway, battery-electric cars now dominate: in 2023, 85% were fully electric, compared to just 8% of plug-in hybrids.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @_HannahRitchie.)

items.title: Chile produced 9.4% of its primary energy from solar in 2023 — the highest in any country that year. According to the Energy Institute’s Statistical...
items.description_html:
Chile produced 9.4% of its primary energy from solar in 2023 — the highest in any country that year.

According to the Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy, in 2023, Chile produced 9.4% of its primary energy from solar sources, the highest share in any country. When we look at electricity alone, solar produced 20% of the total.

This marks a trend of continued year-on-year growth in a country that, just a decade earlier, generated almost no electricity from solar.

Chile's growth has been faster than that of other solar champions like Spain and Australia, where the adoption of these technologies started earlier.

Much of Chile's solar energy is captured in the Atacama Desert. This region, in Northern Chile, receives the highest level of sunlight exposure in the world and is home to Latin America’s first solar thermal plant.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @EOrtizOspina.)

items.title: New @mercatus Policy Paper Alert: 📢 Did you know that 95% of India’s industrial units employ fewer than 10 workers. One of the main reasons Indian...
items.description_html:
New @mercatus Policy Paper Alert: 📢

Did you know that 95% of India’s industrial units employ fewer than 10 workers. One of the main reasons Indian firms are reluctant to scale is prohibitively costly labor regulation.

In this paper, @kadambari_shah and I survey India's labor laws, and find that most labor regulation is too onerous, tends to micromanage factory operations through uncertain enforcement by a labor inspection system, preventing firms from remaining competitive. Second, most of the laws are imposed on firms with as few as 10 employees, disincentivizing firm growth and large-scale employment.

TL;DR: India's labor laws are do too much, too soon in a firm’s life cycle.

We recommend streamlining labor regulations, removing outdated laws, overhauling the inspection system, and judiciously applying criminal penalties. While these reforms contain various political economy challenges, a second-best alternative could be increasing thresholds of existing laws to 1,000 workers for most labor laws and 10,000 for industrial disputes to foster large-scale firms, boost India’s manufacturing sector, and enhance its global competitiveness.

https://the1991project.com/writing/papers/why-indian-firms-dont-scale-labor-edition

items.title: Vaccines account for 40% of the decline in infant mortality over the last 50 years. Infant mortality rates have plummeted over the last 50 years. Glob...
items.description_html:
Vaccines account for 40% of the decline in infant mortality over the last 50 years.

Infant mortality rates have plummeted over the last 50 years. Globally, they’ve fallen by over two-thirds, from around 10% in 1974 to less than 3% today.

A recent study — published in The Lancet by Andrew Shattock and an international team of researchers — estimates that 40% of this decline has been driven by increased access to crucial vaccines.

The chart shows the actual reduction in infant mortality rates with vaccination (in blue) and the researchers’ estimates for a hypothetical scenario in which vaccines wouldn’t have been rolled out (in red).

Based on these figures, vaccines are estimated to have saved 150 million children over the last 50 years.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @_HannahRitchie.)

items.title: The average worker in wealthy countries now works only about half as many hours per year as in the late 19th century. Based on data from economic hist...
items.description_html:
The average worker in wealthy countries now works only about half as many hours per year as in the late 19th century.

Based on data from economic historians Michael Huberman and Chris Minns, the average worker used to work between 2,700 and 3,500 hours per year, which is about 50 to 70 hours each week.

Recently, using data from the Penn World Table, workers worked about half that amount, between 1,300 and 1,800 hours a year, or about 25 to 35 hours a week.

This decrease has come from working fewer hours each day, fewer days each week, and fewer weeks each year.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @bbherre.)

items.title: Since we whine so much about banking services, must give credit where credit is due. ⛔️Had a fraud transaction hit my CC in the middle of the night ...
items.description_html:
Since we whine so much about banking services, must give credit where credit is due.

⛔️Had a fraud transaction hit my CC in the middle of the night a couple of days back. Bank called to verify but I didn’t pick up.

⛔️Clearly bank systems detected that the transaction was atypical for me and unlikely given time of transaction and they probably had enough data to know card was not out of India.

⛔️Bank put card on temporary block, till I woke up and confirmed it was a fraudulent transaction. Transaction was reversed, new card issued when I confirmed the card transaction was fraudulent.

Thank you, unnamed bank.

Read 3-4 concalls every day

items.title: There are two ways to produce seafood: catch fish in the wild or farm your own. Seafood farming is often called “aquaculture”. Aquaculture is domina...
items.description_html:
There are two ways to produce seafood: catch fish in the wild or farm your own. Seafood farming is often called “aquaculture”. Aquaculture is dominated by the farming of fish, but also includes other organisms, such as crustaceans and aquatic plants.

Aquaculture has grown rapidly over the last few decades. In fact, as the chart shows, it has overtaken wild catch since 2013.

This has relieved some pressure on wild fish stocks: if this increased demand for fish had been satisfied by wild catch, then many more would be severely overexploited.

(This Daily Data Insight was written by @_HannahRitchie.)

items.title: In 1820, only 1 in 10 people over the age of 15 could read. Today, the corresponding global literacy rate — the share of adults aged 15 and older who...
items.description_html:
In 1820, only 1 in 10 people over the age of 15 could read. Today, the corresponding global literacy rate — the share of adults aged 15 and older who can read and write — is 87%. That means more than 5 billion people can read and write today, compared to fewer than 100 million two centuries ago.

The chart shows the rising global literacy rate over time. The data combines historical estimates from academic historians with more recent statistics from UNESCO.

As we can see, widespread literacy is a recent achievement. This trend underlines a huge achievement, but it’s important to remember that there’s still much progress ahead of us. In many schools, children learn very little, and many still do not attend school.

This Data Insight was written by @EOrtizOspina.

items.title: Capital gains changes in #Budget2024: 1) Only two holding periods: 12 and 24 months 2) Gold, debentures, bonds will be 24M (along with unlisted shares...
items.description_html:
Capital gains changes in #Budget2024:
1) Only two holding periods: 12 and 24 months
2) Gold, debentures, bonds will be 24M (along with unlisted shares and RE)
3) All listed securities (incl bonds) will be 12M
4) STCG increased from 15% to 20%
5) All LTCG is 12.5%. No indexation anymore. This is important. Even for real estate!
6) LTCG upto 1.25 lakh is tax free (from 1 lakh earlier)

These changes are only from July 23 onwards. Meaning whatever you sold till today was under the older tax, even in this financial year.

LAN, Cabling, Construction companies are advertised as data center stock. Networking devices that are traded for a single digit margins are data center stocks. items.title: How P/E Expansion Works💹 Understand this for spotting future multibaggers💸 This is the main reason why stocks give multibagger returns:🔖👇 ...
items.description_html:
How P/E Expansion Works💹

Understand this for spotting future multibaggers💸

This is the main reason why stocks give multibagger returns:🔖👇

P/E Ratio = Share Price / EPS
Initial
- Share Price - 100
- P/E - 10
- EPS - 10

1⃣Now imagine P/E expanding to 20, EPS doubling in the next 3 years:

After 3 Years
- P/E - 20
- EPS - 20
- Share Price - 400
- Returns - 4X

2⃣Now imagine P/E expanding to 100, EPS doubling in the next 3 years:

After 3 Years
- P/E - 100
- EPS - 20
- Share Price - 2000
- Returns - 20X

3⃣Now imagine earnings expanding by 5X, Exit P/E - 100:

After 3 Years
- EPS - 50
- P/E - 100
- Share Price - 5000
- Returns - 50X

This is how to generate multibagger returns.
Understand and apply the maths.
If you have found a multibagger, just keep riding.

P/E expansion happens in a bull market.
P/E contraction happens in a bear market.

It's very painful to handle a bear market.
Make as much money as you can in a bull market.
Once the bear market comes, the portfolio will be down by 40-60 %.

items.title: This is not a great market to keep harping about valuation. Nearly everyone who's been around longer than 5 years knows that many stocks are wildly ov...
items.description_html:
This is not a great market to keep harping about valuation. Nearly everyone who's been around longer than 5 years knows that many stocks are wildly overvalued. But those with decades of experience know that stocks can remain overvalued and get even more so.

It's time to be aware that you're all riding a tiger. If you're 10% cash, you're not in cash. That is 90% invested. And if you're even in "largecaps" or "defensives" that will still mean you will fall like crazy when you do; in fact the "offensives", i.e. everything other than what you own, could go up another 50% before falling, and end up bottoming out higher than where you end up.

There is zero predicting ability on stock movements right now. It's a mad market, and will remain one until....it's not. The point isn't to keep warning people that it's getting too hot. I've heard this from last December and we're up some 30% or more since. It could fall tomorrow, and it could fall 10 months from now. The better thing to warn people is: stay nimble, and stop loving your stocks. When markets fall, even the ones that give you the most smiles today might need a harsh goodbye.

But till then, accept all the incoming awesome upmoves that you cannot explain. You don't have to explain everything good that happens to you, and you probably don't deserve it, but you're getting it anyway. Happiness doesn't need entitlement - it just needs the ability to sit back and enjoy it while it lasts.

items.title: Semiconductors The Indian Semiconductor market is expected to grow 20-25 % over the next 10 years. Allied sectors will benefit from the semiconductor ...
items.description_html:
Semiconductors

The Indian Semiconductor market is expected to grow 20-25 % over the next 10 years.

Allied sectors will benefit from the semiconductor ecosystem.

The first major fab in India is being set up by Tata Electronics in Gujarat.

Key companies in this area include Kaynes Technology, CG Power, HCL Tech, SPEL Semiconductor.

Semiconductor manufacturing processes require high-purity gases and water, which are the second-biggest material expenditure after silicon.

Linde India, Everest Kanto, Aeroflex Industries, Inox India, and VA Tech Wabag could be the key suppliers to semiconductor companies.

Valuations must be checked independently; most of them are very expensive.

Source: MoneyControl

items.title: According to the most recent data from the Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy, India now consumes more coal than the continents of...
items.description_html:
According to the most recent data from the Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy, India now consumes more coal than the continents of Europe and North America combined.

The chart shows this was not the case until recently. Coal consumption in Europe and North America was high for a long time but has significantly decreased in recent decades. At the same time, India’s consumption has steadily increased.

India has industrialized and is growing rapidly. It has a huge demand for cheap energy, and the country’s abundant coal reserves are being used to meet it.

On a per-capita basis, coal consumption in India has only just passed levels in either region. That’s after centuries of higher consumption in North America and Europe.

(This Data Insight was written by @EOrtizOspina and Pablo Rosado.)

items.title: Energy demand in China has increased rapidly over the last few decades due to rising incomes and industrialization. The country now uses about the sam...
items.description_html:
Energy demand in China has increased rapidly over the last few decades due to rising incomes and industrialization.

The country now uses about the same amount of energy per person as the European Union. You can see this in this chart, with new data from the Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy.

This measure of primary energy is based on the substitution method, which tries to account for the inefficiencies of fossil fuels compared to renewables.

Note that this does not account for energy embedded in traded goods, so some of this increase in China has come from producing goods exported to other countries.

(This Data Insight was written by @_HannahRitchie and Pablo Rosado.)

items.title: Cheat Code for Multi bagger :- - Starting valuations: 15-25 P/E Lower the valuations higher the upside. - Revenue growth: At least 25% This means 2x r...
items.description_html:
Cheat Code for Multi bagger :-

- Starting valuations: 15-25 P/E
Lower the valuations higher the upside.

- Revenue growth: At least 25%
This means 2x revenue in 3 years.

- Levers for margin expansion
Operating leverage must play out.
PAT growth > REV growth.

If revenue doubles, PAT must increase by at least 3-5x.

Voila! You have found a multi bagger.

For all the stocks I add to my portfolio, I try to find the same criteria.

Observe the pattern
Do not overpay
Add only those stocks which fit in this framework
Read concalls, Investor PPT, Management Interview

One Multi bagger with right allocation can change your life.
Bookmark It

items.title: According to the latest Global Burden of Disease Study — published last month by the Institute of Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME) — COVID-19 wa...
items.description_html:
According to the latest Global Burden of Disease Study — published last month by the Institute of Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME) — COVID-19 was the third leading cause of death in 2021, after cardiovascular diseases and cancer.

These estimates suggest that COVID-19 was responsible for around eight million deaths in 2021. In many countries across South America and sub-Saharan Africa, the IHME reports that it was the leading cause of death.

Global improvements in healthcare have led to a steady reduction in the death rate from infectious diseases in recent decades, but the COVID-19 pandemic has reversed this trend.

(This Data Insight was written by
@f_spooner.)

items.title: One of the most transformative changes in technology over the last few decades has been the massive drop in the cost of clean energy. Solar photovolta...
items.description_html:
One of the most transformative changes in technology over the last few decades has been the massive drop in the cost of clean energy. Solar photovoltaic costs have fallen by 90% in the last decade, onshore wind by 70%, and batteries by more than 90%.

These technologies have followed a “learning curve” called Wright’s Law. This states that the cost of technology falls consistently as the cumulative production of that technology increases.

The chart shows the perfect example of this for solar power. This data comes from the International Renewable Agency, Greg Nemet, and Doyne Farmer & François Lafond.

On the horizontal axis, we have the cumulative installed capacity of solar panels, and on the vertical axis, the cost. Both are measured on logarithmic scales, and the trend follows a straight line. That means the fall in cost has been exponential.

Costs have fallen by around 20% every time the global cumulative capacity doubles. Over four decades, solar power has transformed from one of the most expensive electricity sources to the cheapest in many countries.

(This Data Insight was written by
@_HannahRitchie)

items.title: If the BJP returns for a third term, as exit polls predict, manufacturing will be a big focus for the government. It’s not a new imperative for India...
items.description_html:
If the BJP returns for a third term, as exit polls predict, manufacturing will be a big focus for the government.

It’s not a new imperative for India — we’ve struggled to grow the share of manufacturing for decades and for many reasons.

At a policy level — issues with land, labour and tariffs.

At an operational level — customs processes, tax uncertainties for complex supply chains, contract enforcement.

It will take a lot to come together for India to try and rival the Chinese economic miracle, led by manufacturing.

I report, with Aftab Ahmed.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-modi-plans-post-election-reforms-rival-chinese-manufacturing-2024-06-03/

items.title: Great concall by #E2ENetworks. After a pause last Q, it was great to hear from the promoters. Some of the key points I noted: 1. The first is capex fo...
items.description_html:
Great concall by #E2ENetworks. After a pause last Q, it was great to hear from the promoters. Some of the key points I noted:

1. The first is capex for FY25. Plan is to do 800cr capex which will be funded through a combination of banking financing, vendor financing & equity raise. This capex represents the incremental demand company sees and notices no risk in paying off the debt, if any

2. A lot of capex came in stream and current quarter's MRR has some contribution from that additional capex. However, in next 2 Qs the additional capex will fully come in and with this management is confident of clocking 14-16cr of MRR. And their capacity, including additional capex, is fully booked already!

3. Let me clear this once - The co is primarily focused on India but they will eventually server EMEA & US markets (remember they run training & inference on a single core because of this market focus, we talked about this in last concall)

4. The outage (a decadal black swan event as per management) was caused by the vendor who hosts their infra. The vendor team started with UPS without being fully charged when there was a power outage and this caused power surge and damaged some equipment. However, before SLA, co restored the service since they have additional infra on site

5. Was good to see one of their clients join the call & appreciating their service

In sum, as I have been saying this all along, this is never about the infra. And management echoes this. Nobody buys a raw GPU anymore. You need a while SW ecosystem around. Nobody can just go buy a GPU and say let me compete in open market. This is where #E2E has the edge. It remains a SW driven compute provider!

items.title: Good set of results from E2E.. Growth Growth Growth is what this result shows all around.. 1. Quarterly revenue growth of 23% in Q4 vs an avg of 10-11...
items.description_html:
Good set of results from E2E.. Growth Growth Growth is what this result shows all around..

1. Quarterly revenue growth of 23% in Q4 vs an avg of 10-11% qoq growth in the first 3 qoq results..

2. ⁠Company has done a massive capex of ~140-150 cr in H2 (most of it coming in Q4, as suggested by the huge increase in Q4 depreciation number)..

3. Typically company runs at an asset turn of 60-70%, won’t be surprised if company adds around 90cr revenue in FY25 and reaches 180-190 cr revenue number in FY25 itself..

4. ⁠A part of the capex was financed using ~100 cr of debt, therefore the increase in finance cost in Q4..

5. ⁠From next quarter expecting a significant increase in revenues as this massive capex comes online (while the growth in dep, interest cost and other expenses will be at a much lower %, as Q4 results factors in most of these increased costs)..

Expecting 180 cr revenue and atleast 90 cr EBITDA in FY25.. Stock trades around 17 times EV/EBITDA FY25..

Get ready for massive revenue growth ahead!!

Tons of credits to my partner in scheme of things @saumyakothari91 for a detailed analysis.

items.title: Investors lost almost 99% of their wealth in BCG and Varanium. So some thoughts on mistakes...! Investing Lessons from Fraud at Brightcom & Varanium C...
items.description_html:
Investors lost almost 99% of their wealth in BCG and Varanium. So some thoughts on mistakes...!

Investing Lessons from Fraud at Brightcom & Varanium Cloud

1. Caution on small companies making big promises on hot new biz (AI, ML etc) where they don't have experience and resources.

2. Stocks are discussed too much on social media by a particular set of people might have an interest in the stock that's why you are seeing one-sided updates (only positive) of the company

3. Don't just buy because some big investor has bought (his/ her allocation could be less than 1% and your allocation going beyond 40% -60% is scary). They can also go wrong.

4. Low PE does not mean its undervalued.

5. Doing biz or most biz via foreign subsidiaries is the big problem. BCG have zero focus on India but for its peers India is big market. How can media tech company India could not be big market?? does not make sense

6. Don't fall for it just because Index Funds are buying.

7. Don't believe blindly what everything management is saying. BCG lied about its management in Investor PPt itself.

8. Try to connect dots.. if so many negatives or fishy things better to avoid them.

9. Don't catch falling knife

10. Explosive Growth in P&L without underlying growth in Cash Flows and BS is another disaster.

11. Frequent resignations in KMPs and Auditors is another recipe for disaster.

12. Selling visions and guidance via press releases does not give confidence.

13. Signing MoUs, and deals with unknown companies might be fancy but just on paper.

14. Promoters are selling, just accept it. Don't try to neglect other stories like fundraising ki vajah se holding kam hua ya SHP mai error hai.

15. Background of KMPs if looks fishy avoid it.

16. The degree and the impact of governance issues needs to be check on overall business and fundamentals. Can ignore if small ...

17. Rise of retail participation in a stock could be another sign of storified stock

18. Asset Allocation is very important, don't buy the multi-bagger stocks on day 1. Mutlibagger Stocks takes years to create Multibagger Wealth...

Kindly maximise the reach of this tweet to save investors ....!

Remember two golden rules

A. Capital Protection is much more important than Capital Appreciation
B. Read Business, Not Stock Prices

items.title: 🚨 Nandan Nilekani says that India’s path in AI (artificial intelligence) is different. Indian startups have to focus on applications and use cases...
items.description_html:
🚨 Nandan Nilekani says that India’s path in AI (artificial intelligence) is different.

Indian startups have to focus on applications and use cases rather than building the biggest large language models (LLMs) like Silicon Valley startups.

“Let those people with capital, and who have better chips do that,” he said.

“Our advantage currently lies not in compute, cloud, or chips. 𝐎𝐮𝐫 𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐩𝐨𝐩𝐮𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐚𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬. 𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐡𝐲 𝐰𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐛𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐝𝐨𝐰𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐢𝐧𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐑𝐬 100 𝐭𝐨 𝐑𝐬 1……𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐭𝐨 𝐝𝐫𝐚𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐫𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐀𝐈. 𝐖𝐞 𝐜𝐚𝐧'𝐭 𝐝𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐢𝐭 𝐭𝐨 𝐚 𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐮𝐧𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐰𝐞 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐛𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐨 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐞 1 𝐫𝐮𝐩𝐞𝐞 𝐩𝐞𝐫 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧.”

"Winners in AI in India will be those who meet customers where they are," Nilekani said at an event by @PeoplePlusAI earlier this week. These are some comments that I found interesting from his chat.

Startups and companies should leverage AI’s capabilities to personalize products at scale, which has not been possible earlier, according to Nilekani.

“We are moving to a world where instead of you adjusting to the technology, the technology will adjust to you. It will adjust to you as a person, adjust to your skills, adjust your talents, adjust, your capabilities, adjust your language, and your skills……what we have found is whenever we make it easier for technology to adjust to you then our usage goes up,” he said.

Nilekani mentioned that companies should leverage the combination of digital public infrastructure (DPI) and AI. DPI is the combination of Aadhaar, UPI, Account Aggregator (AA), and ONDC open-source software.

This includes examples like leveraging local language capabilities of AI platforms like Bhashini and Sarvam to deliver personalized financial services products or offers, by understanding customer's banking profiles through AA.

There was a demo by Pine Labs/Setu of a new chat-based personal finance product.

“We have a unique infrastructure in India where we are building digital public infrastructure for AI. We are building a set of digital public goods for AI. We are building on top of an existing DPI to power AI and therefore accelerating the adoption of AI in India,” said Nilekani.

“And that's what this is a really the unique way that India is doing in AI which is what gives us confidence that we can move very rapidly in this space. Now, we talk about unbundling, but now with AI, we have a chance to bundle again. It was James Clarke of Netscape who said that we spend half our lives unbundling and then the next half re-bundling.”

“This means that all these AI Tools now allow you to put things back together in a very effective way to get the full impact of the solutions. Because for the first time, we can now personalize at scale.”

items.title: No point in trying to guess why Musk visited China and not India. And that's a wrong question to ask. What we should take away is that such setbacks a...
items.description_html: No point in trying to guess why Musk visited China and not India. And that's a wrong question to ask. What we should take away is that such setbacks are a feature of pro-business policies (rather than pro-market ones). In the former, govt is spending time & enery in designing policies that have the pain points of a couple of anchor investors in mind. That always runs the risk of those investors not showing interest. Instead, we probably need to address the structural bottlenecks that stop all kinds of investors and businesses from investing in India. @anupammanur

items.title: RBI says all personal loans will, from Oct 2024, need to carry a Key Financial Statement that has the details of the actual interest rate, including a...
items.description_html:
RBI says all personal loans will, from Oct 2024, need to carry a Key Financial Statement that has the details of the actual interest rate, including all fees/charges of any loan.
* no more hidden fees (can't charge what isn't mentioned)
* digital loans need a look free prepayment period
* colending details, fees of third parties must be disclosed
* you can even ask for receipts for any legal or other charges at actuals

Super new regulation!

(Doesn't apply to credit cards)

https://www.rbi.org.in/Scripts/NotificationUser.aspx?Id=12663&Mode=0

items.title: Real Estate Sector - Sector for next 1-2 Years🟢 Many of the players will do extremely well🔥 Do not miss this golden opportunity of the real esta...
items.description_html:
Real Estate Sector - Sector for next 1-2 Years🟢

Many of the players will do extremely well🔥

Do not miss this golden opportunity of the real estate upcycle🌆

4 Stocks, Important Metrics, Valuations - Everything covered in detail

Bookmark 🔖 for future reference

🔸Sunteck Realty: Market Cap - 5700 Cr (CMP 390)
~ Strong foothold in MMR market.
~ Plans to double GDV in the next 3 years, 30,000 Cr --> 60,000 Cr.
~ 25% Growth, Pre-Sales Guidance of 2000 Cr in FY24.
~ Annual Rental Portfolio, Expansion from 35 Cr Current to 320 Cr by FY27.
~ Average return of rented properties 30%.
~ Rs 16,000 Cr of net surplus to be generated from 7 large projects.
~ Embedded EBITDA 35% margin.
~ Net Debt 45 Cr (0.02 D/E), Net Worth 3028 Cr.
~ Net Cash Surplus FY23 - 428 Cr, Surplus Yield - 22%.
Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2.8 x, Ev/Embedded EBITDA - 10 x

🔸Prestige Estates: Market Cap - 47,000 Cr (CMP 1170)
~ ₹20,000 Cr Pre-Sales in FY24.
~ 15-20% Growth in FY25, Pre-Sales may exceed ₹24,000 Cr in FY25.
~ Annuity Income Portfolio of 3900 Cr by FY28.
~ Targeting ₹5,000 Cr in FY 2025, with the potential to go up to ₹10,000 Cr.
~ 32,000 Cr Top line will be recognized in the next 3 years.
~ 53 Mnsf project in the pipeline 42,000 Cr + 16,000 Cr of Inventory.
~ Aggressive Expansion in New Geographies Mumbai, Delhi NCR.
Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2.35 x.

🔸Max Estate: Market Cap - 4050 Cr (CMP 276)
~ Best plays in Delhi NCR Region expanding its commercial & real estate portfolio.
~ 2200-2400 Cr Pre-Sales in FY25.
~ 15-20% Growth over the next 3-5 Years.
~ Targeting Sales of 4000-4500 Cr by FY29.
~ Rental Portfolio to expand to 300 Cr by FY27-28 and 400-475 Cr by FY29.
~ Plans to add 2 Mn sqft every year across NCR.
~ Estate 128 (First Luxury Residential Project) Sold out, Total Sales Booking 1800 Cr, Total Collections 441 Cr, Topline Contribution from FY28.
~ Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2 x.

🔸Suraj Estates: Market Cap - 1150 Cr (CMP 260)
~ Suraj Estate is a South Central Mumbai-based real estate company focused on redevelopment projects.
~ The company has a legacy of 37 years and has completed 42 projects.
~ Enjoys 40% Margins for Redevelopment projects.
~ Total 29 Projects (13 - Ongoing sites, 16 - Upcoming Projects).
~ 13 Ongoing sites with a pipeline of 7.44 Lakh sqft.
~ GDV of Rs 708 Cr from ongoing projects.
~ Balance Cash flow of Rs 1,458 Cr to flow from FY25-FY29.
~ The company owns 16 land banks in Mumbai and 80% of them already have approvals.
~ 3 Launches in Q4, will exceed FY23 sales value of 635 Cr.
~ High-Cost Debt Repaid, Repaid 285 Cr Debt and 23.5 Cr additional secured debt, Interest Cost will reduce drastically. Net Debt - 219 Cr
- Mcap/Sales < 2

WHY REAL ESTATE?
- Real estate sector is in the middle of a long upcycle.

- Usually, real estate cycles are long and last for 6-8 years.

- Pre-sales growth has been solid for most of the players.

- As the interest cycle may come down in the next 6 months, the real estate sector will be the biggest beneficiary.

- Housing inventories continue to be at record low levels.

Checklist for Real Estate Companies:
- Pre-sales growth guidance of > 20%.
- Increase in Rental portfolio.
- Pipelines across various geography, Healthy Launches Pipeline.
- Leverage at monitorable levels.
- Strong Execution and Track Record.
- Embedded EBITDA Margin, Cashflows, Pre-Sales, Realisations, Collections, Inventory.
- While tracking Real Estate Stocks, Profit & Loss Statement makes no sense.
- At the end what matters the most is Cashflows, It should be monitored like a hawk.

Pre-Sales & Collections are the most important metrics to track.

VALUATIONS
Mcap/Pre-Sales is a better metric to value real estate companies.
Other Metrics - Embedded EBITDA/Mcap, OCF/Mcap

#PRESTIGE #Prestigeestates #SUNTECK #sunteckrealty #SURAJEST #surajestate #MaxEstates #MAXESTATES #RealEstate #stockmarkets #StocksToWatch

items.title: Yesterday, someone from a Life Insurance company called, insisting I should consider this plan. After three calls, I said fine, just tell me what it i...
items.description_html:
Yesterday, someone from a Life Insurance company called, insisting I should consider this plan. After three calls, I said fine, just tell me what it is.

Pay us 1 lakh per year for seven years, she said. In the eighth year, we'll give you back 11 lakh!

Now I have this number machine in my head, and this sounded quite good; I quickly put it in an excel sheet and voila: 11%.

Insurance has a shady thing where you pay at the start of the year, but they pay you at the end. Adjusting for that, 9%.

Who's guaranteeing 9%? I asked for the name of the plan.

"SBI Wealth Builder", she said.

I look it up.

"But it's a ULIP", I say aloud

"Yes, sir. You can take the bond fund option"

"How can you guarantee a ULIP", I ask, actually curious because who knows what has changed for insurers.

"You'll get that much, sir, Bond fund will give 9%"

"Have you gotten 9% in your bond fund?"

"Yes sir, but I'll get back to you with details"

*Click*

She never calls back.

I'm a nerd. So I look it up. Their own bond fund has, even over five years, given just 5.6%, before other charges.

Of course she lied, but the bigger lie was the guarantee. Saying that you will guaranteed receive 9% - in a ULIP - is just plain illegal, misleading and fraudulent. Beware and just say no.

items.title: Amidst all the gloom & crash posts let me just share my experience & learnings of 2008!! Back then the crash was very prolonged & painful. I was new i...
items.description_html:
Amidst all the gloom & crash posts let me just share my experience & learnings of 2008!! Back then the crash was very prolonged & painful. I was new in the market & left market after losing major capital. I came back only after 3yrs. The reason was two fold:
1. Many companies I invested in hardly had any good fundamentals.
2. Some companies were very good though
And, I treated everything the same, panicked.
My father also sold his MF holding in hurry. I stood no chance of recovering because companies lacked strong business but my father's MF holding could have grown multi-fold had he just stayed in.

Long story short, if you are holding good businesses, the only way to fight this crash & win is - staying in! If you've got the 1st act right - good business with good management, just stay in.

If you are holding bad companies, this is a lesson & is definitely not the end of the road! I felt like back in 2008 but now 16yrs later it doesn't feel like. So just staying the course is best solution. Stay positive, learn where possible, and just stay in. You can always make a come back!

items.title: The government finally released a household consumption survey late last evening. This is the first consumption survey since 2011-12 and will also be ...
items.description_html:
The government finally released a household consumption survey late last evening. This is the first consumption survey since 2011-12 and will also be the basis of the CPI review.

Some takeaways:
- Urban consumption expectedly is up more than rural consumption.
- Monthly average rural spending has risen to Rs 3773 in 2022-23 survey from Rs 1430 in 2011-12 survey.
- Share of spending on food has declined suggesting more discretionary spend.
- Within food, share of spending on cereals is down quite sharply. More spending on processed food. This is true both in urban and rural areas.
- Within non-food, there is a jump in share of spending on durables in rural areas, conveyance, rent in urban areas.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indians-spending-less-food-more-discretionary-items-government-survey-2024-02-25/

items.title: I don't think India should ever make minimum support prices a legally required thing. It means instant bankruptcy of the nation for one. And then, a n...
items.description_html:
I don't think India should ever make minimum support prices a legally required thing. It means instant bankruptcy of the nation for one. And then, a no-income-tax paying entity shouldn't get absolute guaranteed income (no one else gets it!).

Instant bankruptcy because currently only a small portion of all rice/wheat/etc is bought, and that too from a few states. Make it guaranteed and there is simply not enough money available. This would be financial suicide for the whole country.

Rich farmers - with any level of income, even 100s of crores - don't pay income tax. (Even corporations in the agri business don't have to) This is unfair.

Lastly, we have an income guarantee scheme (NREGA) which should be all that is needed. (I don't think it helps but it should be for just bare sustenance) This applies to all Indians, not just farmers. We shouldn't guarantee anyone unlimited income - take the risk which means deal with some downside, and then do take the upside.

items.title: In Bull Markets storytelling becomes very common... Every stock/company tells a fascinating story about their future... But only in a bear market - we...
items.description_html:
In Bull Markets storytelling becomes very common... Every stock/company tells a fascinating story about their future...

But only in a bear market - we find which companies were actually a Storified Stock (basically which never did walk the talk)

There is another way to verify whether management is reasonable / honest or not -

Read con-calls - Cross-verify management commentaries with peers or co-relate operational aspects with financial aspect & vice versa

Let's take an example - was reading concall of a steel pipe manufacturer - JTL Industries

JTL on dealer network expansion plans and opportunity size said they have already reached saturation levels as they have around 800 dealers out of total 1100 dealers in India

To verify this whether 800 dealers is saturated number or not? I checked distributors of industry leader - APL
and I found that APL too had 800 around dealers... Hence the commentary of JTL is verified

So there are the few ways along with example of doing Walk The Talk Test of The Management

Disc - No recommendations

items.title: Recently one Ace investor invested in this Microcap "Sanjivani Paranteral" via preferential route. But Bright story in Bull Market has some Dark Side ...
items.description_html:
Recently one Ace investor invested in this Microcap "Sanjivani Paranteral" via preferential route.
But Bright story in Bull Market has some Dark Side as well.

Some Previous Disclosure Lapses by Promoter Ashwani Khemka -
In FY13, Promoter increased stake from 7.95% to 10.10% and disclosed the same with delay of 3,227 days
Similarly, in FY 2016, Promoter sold 11.02% stake via revocation by pledge and disclosed the same with delay of 1783 days.

Both delays disclosed on 19-Aug-21.
Then on 6-May-22, Promoter settled this with SEBI by paying the settlement amt worth 4.95L.

In the last Credit Rating Report also Crisil issued CRISIL D rating due to non-cooperation by company. Company is not sharing any data since 2018, from there in next 5 years, CRISIL issued them with CRISIL D rating.

Old Pending statutory dues unpaid-
Custom Duty worth 1.36 Cr belongs to 2020-21 (Equals to their FY21 PAT)
NMMC Cess worth 50L is payable worth 50L belongs to FY02 to FY13.

Company also has defaulted on Loan, that loan assigned to ARC which company settled.

In Bull Run, Many companies raise funds to survive in the bear phase.

Governance Goldmine - How to Find Frauds?
https://stocktwits.onelink.me/Lo6t/t7acynxn

#Redflag #SanjivaniParanteral

items.title: Ashneer Grover apparently siphoned out funds to his family in the name of recruitment services - a well known mechanism to take money out. So stupidly...
items.description_html:
Ashneer Grover apparently siphoned out funds to his family in the name of recruitment services - a well known mechanism to take money out. So stupidly done too - they backdated invoices with bank accounts that didn't even exist at that date!

Remember news at that time stated that over 53 cr. was paid to such "sham" companies, owned or connected to Ashneer or his wife. These cos didn't even file GST (though they charged it!) which is what brought the whole house down.

I wonder if investors in startups have at least got a policy saying we'll do an annual independent review of recruitment related payments and links to founders...this kind of "taking it out" is fairly common.

https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/startup/ashneer-grover-family-generated-fake-invoices-that-had-bank-account-numbers-created-later-reveals-eow-probe-11745891.html

items.title: Holy moly. This is actual fraud and I found that @Swiggy is doing this even for me! Here's my last order and it adds up to 255.60. But they charge 259...
items.description_html:
Holy moly. This is actual fraud and I found that @Swiggy is doing this even for me! Here's my last order and it adds up to 255.60. But they charge 259?

This stinks. It can't be some random error, it seems to be on purpose to add rs. 3 extra. What's going on?


mas.to /: @Swiggy And it's not Swiggy just stealing a few extra paisa per order.

Eg.
626.57+35.24+2.00+28.00-52.99+33.09 = 671.91

Instead of adding 9 paisa to round up to the whole rupee, @Swiggy charges Rs.3.09 extra instead.

Absolutely no justification for this.

items.title: If you are 20 and start making a monthly SIP of Rs 3184, you will reach 10 Cr corpus at 60 years. But if you waste time till you reach 35, you will ne...
items.description_html:
If you are 20 and start making a monthly SIP of Rs 3184, you will reach 10 Cr corpus at 60 years.

But if you waste time till you reach 35, you will need to do a monthly sip of Rs 30450 (almost 10x) to reach the 10 cr mark.

The message to youngsters is - Experiment, take your risks while you are young, but keep aside your SIP without any compromise.

Also if you start at 35, you will need to set invest a total sum of 91.3 lakhs against investment of 15.28 lakhs by someone who started at 20.

This is the power and magic of compounding when age is on your side.

items.title: This is HUGE! RBI says banks and NBFCs have to release documents of properties and collateral mandatorily within 30 days! * Have to remove encumberanc...
items.description_html:
This is HUGE! RBI says banks and NBFCs have to release documents of properties and collateral mandatorily within 30 days!
* Have to remove encumberance within 30 days, and return docs
* Docs can be returned from any branch, not just home branch of customer
* Loan docs must mention where the borrower can get them back once he repays
* If the bank/NBFC doesn't return docs or release the encumberance charge within 30 days - they pay 5000 rs. per day of delay
* If bank/NBFC loses the docs - they will pay and assist owner in getting docs back or attested copies (but they get another 30 days i.e 60 days before the 5K per day penalty, only if they lose the docs)
* If the loan taker dies, Banks/NBFCs need to have a detailed procedure on how heirs can get original docs.

Applies for even past loans where full repayment happens after December 1, 2023.

This is awesome for homeowners who borrow, but also for collateral based lending like for cars, securities and all the other stuff. And applies to NBFCs also, so housing finance companies can't escape.

Why is Jio financial services, JIOFIN at lower circuit every day? It's one of the pitfalls of having so much money in index funds - forced selling.

The largest Indian mutual fund scheme is the SBI Nifty 50 ETF. It has 160,000 cr. in assets. It tracks the NIFTY 50 Index. Also, if you just add the list of funds that track the Nifty 50, you will see over 270,000 crore in Indian mutual funds that track the Nifty.

In the Nifty is Reliance Industries at a weightage of about 10%. It used to be 11%, but then JIOFIN was demerged out of it, and effectively, 1% of the NIFTY is in JIOFIN.

Because Reliance was in the index, JIOFIN, as a demerger result, is also kept in the index. In a set of rules in March 2023, the NSE said this:
* when an index stock demerges, the demerged stock will be part of the index till it lists
* after it lists, it will still be there for three days after which it will be removed from the index
* if it hits circuits for the first two days, it remains in the index for another two days.

JIOFIN listed on Monday. It hit the lower circuit on Monday and Tuesday, so it will stay in the index till Friday. If it keeps hitting the lower circuit, it will remain in the index until a day when it doesn't hit the circuits and boom, the next day it's out.

Complex? That's because of the index funds. JIOFIN doesn't qualify to be in the index (not enough listed history etc) So it should be removed. But because Index Funds own all the stocks in the Nifty, they will also have JIOFIN through the demerger. Remember all shareholders of Reliance got an equal number of shares of JIOFIN.

You can't remove JIOFIN from the index. Because the funds can't sell JIOFIN. If you removed JIOFIN from the index, the weights of all stocks would change but because Index funds can't sell it, they would have the wrong weights and not be able to track the index properly.

So the rule explains things: when JIOFIN lists and there's some time to sell it (assuming no circuits) then JIOFIN is removed.

Why is JIOFIN falling? Because these index funds want to sell it - the minute it is out of the lower circuit, it's out of the index so they need to be out of it by then.

Basically, they don't care if JIOFIN is a good co. It's like hello, it's not in the index, so I sell boss, don't care about anything else. This is "forced selling".

Now how much is there? Consider that 270,000 cr. is in Indian index funds (apart from active mutual funds that hold Reliance - those funds don't have to sell and can hold it for longer)

1% of that is 2,700 crores. That's the quantity that will be forced to sell, roughly. At Rs. 224 per share, that is roughly 12 crore shares that index funds must sell.

The current sell quantities on the exchange (NSE) are around 14 crore shares. Only 42 lakh shares have actually traded. Until this selling is absorbed, we'll see these lower circuits. It doesn't matter how good or bad the company is.

Disclosure: we own it.

items.title: If you are bullish on India, you should be double bullish on Uttar Pradesh. As per RBI August bulletin, UP has the highest share of projects financed ...
items.description_html:
If you are bullish on India, you should be double bullish on Uttar Pradesh. As per RBI August bulletin, UP has the highest share of projects financed by Banks in FY22-23 at 16.2%. This was just 1.1% a decade back!

UP has 20% of India's population. This is more than the total population of all but 5 countries in the world! When you imagine the scale and the base effect, you will see massive investment opportunities.

Here's how an investor can approach and play this:

1. Figure out which Infra and building materials companies are strong in UP and doing projects. Eg: Why PNC Infratech is strong in UP, which companies are doing steel pipe and gas pipe layout?

2. There were ~12 lakh ITRs filed this year vs 1.4 lakh in 2014. So income levels are rising and consumption will rise more. Figure out which consumption oriented companies are strong in UP. Apparel, footwear, alcohol, etc.

3. NBFCs and MFIs which are strong in UP. A lot of people will be setting own small businesses. Women setting up small stores, etc. Which lender is catering to them?

I feel the first stage of UP's progress was laid in bringing law and order and hard measures wherever needed. I don't want to bring any political bias, but there's a reason why Yogi Adityanath is considered the most important minister in BJP. Now that UP is moving from lawlessness to progress, it will steer a large part of India's growth. Many companies will go from 500 crore to 20000 crore mcap in next 5-10 years.

As an investor, our job is to keep biases aside and focus on making the right investments. Let's just focus on that.

items.title: Varanium Cloud - Saga of - Capital Misallocation | IPO Fund Mis-utilization | Discrepancy in Cash Flows | Dividend Saga 1. The company raised 40 crore...
items.description_html:
Varanium Cloud - Saga of - Capital Misallocation | IPO Fund Mis-utilization | Discrepancy in Cash Flows | Dividend Saga

1. The company raised 40 crore via IPO in Sept-22 and as of March-23, it has unutilized 15 crore from IPO funds
Now again company raised whopping 1250 crores for purpose of some acquisition (not announced yet)

2. Company has given dividend 4 times leading to outgo of INR 16 crore interestingly company has not paid any dividend before IPO.
3. Out of this 3 crores has been paid by March-23 still, there is no record of such payout in Cash Flow Statement (in Lakhs)
4. Company has negligible cash flows from other means and has negative cash flow from operations, so basically they don't have any other cash sources to pay such high amount of dividend apart from utilizing IPO funds. A possible case of IPO fund mis-utilization?

Following remain questions:
1. Why company is regularly paying dividend at one side and raising money at other side?
2. Why company wants to raise fund if IPO fund remain unutilized?
3. Why dividend paid till March-23 is not reflecting in Cash flows?
4. How company funding its huge dividend payout?

#redflag #corporategovernance #varaniumcloud #varanium

items.title: Personally went through this...Mom went to the court house for two years once every two months to evict a tenant who refused to pay rent after the fir...
items.description_html:
Personally went through this...Mom went to the court house for two years once every two months to evict a tenant who refused to pay rent after the first month. He also filed a case saying henchmen used just like that. Had done this with other people too.

It took two years to get a court order, after which it took another three months to actually evict...the fraud renter left on the last day before the cops were brought in.

After that mom sold all real estate and now lives in the only house she owns. We've even had a piece of land taken over by goondas and then settled.

Real estate is not something that works for us. More money has been made in markets with far lesser hassle, by us.


Neil Borate: This still happens. Very few people talk about this risk in #realestate.

items.title: The most interesting thing in whole Reliance Retail Saga is here👇 Everyone's buzzing about Reliance paying a fancy premium on the valuation done by...
items.description_html:
The most interesting thing in whole Reliance Retail Saga is here👇

Everyone's buzzing about Reliance paying a fancy premium on the valuation done by BDO and E&Y.

But guess what?

Let me tell u sneaky tax twist too!

When a company having accumulated profits, and reduces its capital. It will be considered as Deemed Dividend and taxed like a dividend section 2(22)(d), as per the slab rate.
Yikes! 😬 But wait!

Reliance isn't one to back down. They're cleverly compensating for it by paying that premium!
Let's crunch the numbers: 1362 x 0.65 (considering 35% Tax)= 885! That's the magic valuation by E&Y!

If Reliance didn't pay the premium, shareholders would be left with just - 885 x 0.65 = 575 in their bank accounts! 😱🤯

Coming up with full story and detailed Thread on Reliance Retail. 🔍
Stay tuned 🍿

@deepakshenoy @govindethiraj
#Reliance #RelianceRetail

Our own M&A and deal terms were complicated because of our singapore structure.

items.title: Since everyone's making a thread about SVB, here's my contribution. Silicon Valley Bank is an old boring conservative bank. It is in trouble not becau...
items.description_html:
Since everyone's making a thread about SVB, here's my contribution. Silicon Valley Bank is an old boring conservative bank. It is in trouble not because of bad lending practices, but because of a combination of
- strange way to invest short term money
- announcing a capital raise when another bank went bust
- and then telling people not to panic

SVB is not the modern Enron. It's not the modern Lehman. It's the modern LTCM, that thing that invested in good things and still (nearly) died.

SVB got a deluge of deposits after the Covid led low interest rate regime, to nearly $180 bn. Mostly startups, which parked money with it at zero interest. They technically should just park this money in something super liquid and stable, like T-Bills because oh pay out 0% and get even 0.25% and you're good, no?

But no because a) they wanted more and b) maybe can do safe but longer term to get more?

So they invested about half of this money in Mortgage Based Securities (MBS). Which matures typically in 10+ years (average mortgages are 30 yr). They got 1.5% which is good when TBills yield 0.25%

But the Fed hiked rates like crazy and, importantly, stopped buying MBS. The MBS market sulked and yields went to as much as 7%. When yields go up from 1.5% to 7%, the price falls huge. Plus in MBS, it also means no one pays back principal early (because you do not refinance a mortgage when the rates are higher) meaning that those excel sheets which looked at the past and said oh, you'll make back your money in about 10 years were, uhm, wrong.

All in all, massive portfolio that's long term in nature. Liquid indeed, but at a much much lower price today. Losses are huge, but they can hold to maturity and get back their money (at 1.5%) What happens next?

They don't NEED to sell MBS. It might be a very decent lending book, and losses might not be much even if there are defaults since you have a house in a roaring property market. So it wasn't "bad lending".

But what happened was that their customers could withdraw money when they wanted. Which happened in the last year through cash burn, I suppose, and they needed to rejig their portfolio.

They sold $21 bn of securities (including treasuries and MBS) for a loss of $1.8 bn. Given that deposits were still $169bn in end-Feb (From $181 in Dec 2021) it's like they took this decision to just reduce asset duration and take that loss.

Just that and it wouldve been fine. They decided to even raise more capital. Exactly at the time that another bank, Silvergate, was in super-distress because it did the exact wrong thing: make bad loans. When a bank's going down and another bank says:
- oh we made some losses
- oh we need more money

Shit hits the fan. Then, if you tell people that no, we sold very good loans, not bad loans, please don't panic, guess what happens? People panic.

Stocks down 60% and VCs are telling their startups to get their money out. Perception, in a banking system, is reality.

The steel pipe itself has a wall of 4.1 centimeters (1.6 inches), and it's coated with another 6-11 cm of steel-reinforced concrete. Each section of the pipe weighs 11 tonnes, which goes to 24-25 tonnes after the concrete is applied.

Price caps on 50 per cent of private college medical seats is a terrible policy. It will lead to fewer and smaller private colleges. The fee of the remaining 50 per cent will rise further. Instead of increasing the total number of seats, we will end up creating more scarcity.


PMO India: कुछ दिन पहले ही सरकार ने एक और बड़ा फैसला लिया है जिसका बड़ा लाभ गरीब और मध्यम वर्ग के बच्चों को मिलेगा।

हमने तय किया है कि प्राइवेट मेडिकल कॉलेजों में आधी सीटों पर सरकारी मेडिकल कॉलेज के बराबर ही फीस लगेगी: PM @narendramodi

items.title: And this is confirmed: European utilities are set to buy tomorrow more Russian natural gas (from Gazprom) via Ukraine pipelines. Yes, you read that ri...
items.description_html:
And this is confirmed:

European utilities are set to buy tomorrow more Russian natural gas (from Gazprom) via Ukraine pipelines.

Yes, you read that right: Europe will be buying more natural gas. From Russia. Via Ukraine. Tomorrow.


Javier Blas: I'm hope to be proven badly wrong on this:

I believe Europe will buy more (no less) Russian natural gas in the next few days. Yes, that's right: more. That's capitalism in times of war. The reason is linked with how Gazprom import contract pricing is structured.

At Rs 64,365 cr ($8.6 bn+) at the upper-end Zomato will be more valuable than the combined value of 👇

Jubilant (Domino's - Rs 41,200 cr)
Burger King India (Rs 6,700 cr)
Westlife (McDonald's - Rs 8,500 cr)
BBQ Nation (Rs 3,500 cr)

Can't Imagine this in India yet. This has to change, which will have snowballing effect on overall mfg

items.title: Today you may tag me for your winning stocks. Tomorrow you may blame me for your losers. It’s your risk/reward game. Play it your way. Remember it’s...
items.description_html:
Today you may tag me for your winning stocks. Tomorrow you may blame me for your losers. It’s your risk/reward game. Play it your way. Remember it’s always your capital at risk. Your conviction. Your goals.

Kindly refrain from excessive flattery and tagging. Thanks.


Antifragile Thinking: Humble request - please don't shower any credit (or discredit) for interim gains (or losses) in your holdings. You know your personal goals. You know your #risk profile. You know what you're doing. You're mature enough to act sensibly. Refrain from instant gratification. Thanks.

items.description_html:
I try to use this stat in my public policy 101 lectures. The single-largest expenditure item in GoI's expenditure profile is:

interest on previous loans. Interest alone, not principal. 23 paise out of ₹1. Talk about intergenerational inequity.

Harsh Goenka: A senior NCP leader once told me ‘We are a aloo party’.
I asked him ‘What does that mean?’
He replied ‘Like aloo can go with matar, gobhi, pyaz, anything for that matter, our party can also blend with any party’.

Now, I understood what he wanted to say !

items.description_html: Re 7/ Bigger question: Why is Trump Admin using bilateral surpluses as measure of success? Normal in trade to have deficits w/ some countries. Trade deficits go up in good economic times. Mexico’s surplus in ’94-’95 grew as its economy crashed. Not worth it. http://bit.ly/piie_3waystrade

items.title: Re 6/ But we got main forecast right. US gains big on net from #NAFTA from growing two-way trade. N. America more competitive globally. Auto sector ga...
items.description_html: Re 6/ But we got main forecast right. US gains big on net from #NAFTA from growing two-way trade. N. America more competitive globally. Auto sector gained from regional supply chains, agriculture grew, migration fell, Mexican energy sector developed. http://bit.ly/piie_NAFTA20

items.title: Re 5/ Yes, PIIE forecast for trade surplus following #NAFTA was wrong because we did not foresee collapse in Mexican investment/growth in ‘94-95. Was...
items.description_html: Re 5/ Yes, PIIE forecast for trade surplus following #NAFTA was wrong because we did not foresee collapse in Mexican investment/growth in ‘94-95. Was mistake to try to precisely forecast that or net job creation. We learned from our analytical mistakes 25 years ago.

items.title: Re 2/ Here is the PIIE study @ErinBurnett referenced on @CNN showing that a past US tariff on Chinese tires saved 1,200 jobs, but cost 3,700 retail jo...
items.description_html: Re 2/ Here is the PIIE study @ErinBurnett referenced on @CNN showing that a past US tariff on Chinese tires saved 1,200 jobs, but cost 3,700 retail jobs. US consumers paid $900,000 per job saved. Data calculations available for replication. http://bit.ly/piie_USTireTariffs

items.title: Re 3/ WTO rules allow tariffs in select cases. US protections already exist for steel—it’s why only 3% of US steel imports are from China. The new t...
items.description_html: Re 3/ WTO rules allow tariffs in select cases. US protections already exist for steel—it’s why only 3% of US steel imports are from China. The new tariffs will raise costs for US auto, other manufacturers, where 40x more Americans work than in steel mills. http://bit.ly/piie_SteelProtection

items.title: Re 4/ Tariffs are no free lunch. US will lose market share in autos and manufactures, raise prices for our consumers, and other countries may retaliat...
items.description_html: Re 4/ Tariffs are no free lunch. US will lose market share in autos and manufactures, raise prices for our consumers, and other countries may retaliate, destroying other jobs. Instead, the US should mobilize allies to pressure China to reform. http://bit.ly/piie_HowNotTo
